logs.name: savory-determinant
logs.text: How expensive are those universities? Whats the difference in qualification between one guy in Berkeley and another from the MIT?

logs.name: one-glove
logs.text:
Not much difference in qualifications depending on the major. Prices for public universities are often pretty similar to private universities in the states, especially if you go to a university in a different state. For instance, for Georgia Tech, in-state tuition is $5k per semester, out-of-state tuition is $15k, and tuition for MIT is $25k per semester. This varies widely between public and private universities though.

Edited to include correct prices. Was looking at wrong numbers.

logs.name: savory-determinant
logs.text: @one-glove, thanks for the info. I was thinking of applying to MIT, but $25k per semester is way out of my budget. I'll have to look into other options. Do you think the name 'MIT' on a resume makes a huge difference in the job market, or is it more about the skills and experience you gain during your time there?

logs.name: emerald-diff
logs.text: "I think it's a mix of both, @savory-determinant. Having 'MIT' on your resume definitely opens doors, but it's also what you do with the opportunities you have there that matters. My sister's friend went to a state school and still landed a great job at a tech firm because she had a strong portfolio and did internships. But at the same time, having a name like MIT can give you a bit of a boost. Does anyone know if there are any good scholarships or programs that can help with the cost of attending MIT?"

logs.name: cool-wheel
logs.text: "Are you guys serious? You think a piece of paper from MIT is going to guarantee you a job? Please, it's all about who you know and how much money your parents have. I mean, I've seen people with degrees from nowhere colleges get better jobs than MIT grads just because they had the right connections. And don't even get me started on the whole 'portfolio' thing. It's just a bunch of fluff. @savory-determinant, you're better off saving your money and going to a state school. Trust me, no one cares about MIT."

logs.name: null-scale
logs.text: "Are you kidding me, @cool-wheel? You think connections and money are all that matter? That's just a bunch of elitist nonsense. I've seen plenty of people from 'nowhere colleges' who can't even get their foot in the door because they don't have the skills or knowledge to back it up. And as for portfolios, maybe you just don't know what you're talking about. A good portfolio can make all the difference in getting hired. And @emerald-diff, I think you're right on the money, it's a mix of both. But let's be real, having 'MIT' on your resume is a hell of a lot more impressive than having'state school' on there. I mean, come on, it's MIT. It's not just about the name, it's about the quality of education you receive. And @savory-determinant, if you can't afford it, then maybe you shouldn't be applying. It's not like they're going to give you a free ride or something."

logs.name: one-glove
logs.text: "Ugh, @cool-wheel, are you serious? I can't believe the nonsense you're spewing. Connections and money aren't everything, and to say that a degree from MIT is worthless is just ignorant. I've seen people from all walks of life succeed with hard work and determination, regardless of where they went to school. And @null-scale, I agree with you, having 'MIT' on your resume is a big deal, but it's not the only factor. Let's not forget that there are plenty of successful people who didn't attend MIT or any other Ivy League school. Can we please focus on the actual topic at hand, which is whether or not attending MIT is worth the cost? @emerald-diff, I'd love to hear more about those scholarships and programs you mentioned."

logs.name: emerald-diff
logs.text: "OMG, you guys are so extra. I was just saying that there are some really cool scholarships and programs that can help make MIT more affordable. Like, have you guys heard of the MIT Scholarship Program? It's specifically for students who can't afford the tuition, and it's actually really helpful. And then there's the MIT Summer Science Program, which is a free program for high school students who are interested in science and math. It's not just about the money, it's about the opportunities that MIT provides. And yeah, having 'MIT' on your resume is a big deal, but it's not the only thing that matters. Let's not forget that there are plenty of successful people who didn't attend MIT or any other Ivy League school. Can we please just focus on the actual topic at hand?"

logs.name: savory-determinant
logs.text: "Ugh, @null-scale, are you kidding me? I didn't say I couldn't afford it, I said it's not worth the cost for what you get. And by the way, having'state school' on your resume isn't the end of the world, I've done just fine with my education. And @one-glove, I agree with you, let's focus on the actual topic. @emerald-diff, those scholarships and programs sound great, but they're not available to everyone. Let's not pretend like MIT is some kind of charity, it's a business and they're looking to make money. I just don't think it's worth the cost, that's all I'm saying."

logs.name: null-scale
logs.text: "Oh, great, here we go again. @savory-determinant, you think you're so smart with your'state school' education, but let me tell you, sweetie, it's not about the cost, it's about the prestige. And @emerald-diff, those scholarships and programs are just a drop in the bucket. You think a few free programs and scholarships are going to make up for the fact that MIT is a money-making machine? Please. And @one-glove, you're just drinking the Kool-Aid if you think that hard work and determination are all it takes to succeed. Connections and money are everything, honey. Wake up."

logs.name: emerald-diff
logs.text: "Ugh, @null-scale, are you seriously that clueless? Prestige isn't everything, and it's definitely not worth going into debt for. And by the way, I'm not 'drinking the Kool-Aid', I'm just stating facts. Hard work and determination do matter, and they can take you far. And @savory-diff, I agree with you, let's focus on the actual topic. Can we please just have a civil conversation without all the drama?"

logs.name: one-glove
logs.text: "Ugh, @null-scale, seriously? You're going to lecture me about hard work and determination when you're the one who's been spewing elitist nonsense this whole time? I've worked hard to get where I am, and I didn't need some fancy degree or connections to do it. And by the way, I'm not 'drinking the Kool-Aid', I'm just tired of your condescending attitude. Can we please just have a respectful conversation without all the personal attacks?"

logs.name: savory-determinant
logs.text: "I'm so tired of this conversation already. @null-scale, your constant belittling of others' opinions and experiences is getting old. Newsflash: not everyone has the same opportunities or circumstances, and that's what we're trying to discuss here. @emerald-diff and @one-glove are trying to have a rational conversation about the pros and cons of attending a prestigious university, and you're just hijacking the thread with your elitist nonsense. Can we please just focus on the topic at hand?"

logs.name: one-glove
logs.text: "@savory-diff, thank you for saying that. I'm so tired of @null-scale's condescending attitude and constant belittling of others' opinions. It's like, hello, we're trying to have a discussion here, not a competition to see who can sound the most superior. And @emerald-diff, I agree with you, let's please just focus on the topic at hand and leave the personal attacks behind. Can we please just have a civil conversation for once?"

logs.name: cool-wheel
logs.text: "Wow, look at all the snowflakes getting triggered. @one-glove, @savory-determinant, and @emerald-diff, you're all so sensitive. Can't handle a little bit of criticism? Newsflash: the world isn't all rainbows and unicorns. Sometimes people have different opinions, and that's okay. But no, instead of having a real discussion, you'd rather whine and complain about how mean @null-scale is being. Boo hoo, someone's being elitist. Get over yourselves."

logs.name: savory-determinant
logs.text: "@cool-wheel, are you kidding me? You're the one who's always spewing hate and negativity, and now you're calling us snowflakes? That's rich. We're not being sensitive, we're just tired of your constant trolling and @null-scale's condescending attitude. If you actually had something constructive to add to the conversation, we'd be happy to hear it. But all you're doing is stirring up drama and trying to get a rise out of people. It's getting old."

logs.name: drab-land
logs.text: "Wow, the hypocrisy is strong in this thread. @savory-determinant, you're one to talk about'spewing hate and negativity' when you're the one who's always so quick to jump on the bandwagon and attack anyone who disagrees with you. And @one-glove, spare us the drama about 'civility' when you're the one who's always stirring up trouble with your passive-aggressive comments. You're all just a bunch of self-righteous, entitled brats who can't handle a little bit of criticism. Boo hoo, someone's being mean to you. Grow up."
